tests: 212 passed, 3 deselected (pre-existing Windows cp1252 fixture-encoding failures in tests/test_gems.py + tests/test_item_bases_manual.py — unrelated to this run). 28/28 intent_classifier tests pass.

questions_processed.question: Countess run superstition — does opening the chest affect rune drops? what other D2 superstitions do players have?
questions_processed.improvement_notes: Gap was knowledge content + nothing surfaced for 'superstition / chest' framing. Agent's baseline answer gave generic Countess rune-table info without addressing the chest-superstition question. Added 3 internal strategy cards: (a) Countess chest vs rune-table debunk (chest is independent from the special rune roll), (b) D2 superstitions real-vs-placebo (chest myth, area re-entry myth, class-bias myth, town-portal myth, vs real techniques Meph moat + /players N + MF on killing blow), (c) Countess rune farming levers-that-matter (Hell/players/runs-per-hour vs chest/direction/class). Top strategy hit now directly addresses the chest=placebo claim factually.

regression_check.notes: reddit_1s7nm7p (heralds) INITIALLY regressed: the class+build_ctx heuristic mis-routed it to build_advice instead of mechanics_query because substring 'sin' matched 'session' (class abbreviation for assassin). Root-caused and fixed by switching _CLASS_NAMES detection to word-boundary regex. Post-fix, heralds question correctly routes to mechanics_query and surfaces herald/TZ/sunder facts. reddit_1s96soq (consume demon) still routes to build_advice and surfaces Warlock-consume strategy cards as expected. All 6 tracked passed questions (1rrw27j, 1rz3qt9, 1s46d6b, 1s7nm7p, 1s96soq, 1r4gn0i) verified on post-fix classifier.
